    • @streamd/react-native

    Module @streamd/react-native - v0.0.1

    @streamd/react-native — React Native renderer for streamd token trees.

    @streamd/react-native

    React Native renderer for the @streamd/parser token tree. Uses Text / View / Pressable / Image primitives so the same component works on iOS, Android, and the web (via react-native-web).

    Install

    npm install @streamd/react-native @streamd/parser @streamd/tokens react react-native

    react (18 or 19) and react-native (0.72+) are peer dependencies.

    Render markdown

    import { StreamdMarkdownNative, ThemeProvider } from "@streamd/react-native";
    import { darkTheme } from "@streamd/tokens";

    export function Post({ markdown }: { markdown: string }) {
      return (
        <ThemeProvider theme={darkTheme}>
          <StreamdMarkdownNative source={markdown} parseOptions={{ gfm: true }} />
        </ThemeProvider>
      );
    }

    Link handling

    <StreamdMarkdownNative
      source={markdown}
      onLinkPress={(href) => Linking.openURL(href)}
    />

    Links render as <Pressable> wrapping a <Text> and call onLinkPress when tapped.

    Component overrides

    Every token kind has a matching component you can replace — same shape as @streamd/react but mapped to React Native primitives:

    const components = {
      codeBlock: ({ content, lang }) => (
        <View style={styles.code}>
          <Text style={{ color: pickLangColor(lang) }}>{content}</Text>
        </View>
      ),
    };

    <StreamdMarkdownNative source={md} components={components} />;

    Plugins

    The same plugin pipeline from @streamd/plugins works here:

    import { sanitize, linkAttributes } from "@streamd/plugins";

    <StreamdMarkdownNative
      source={untrusted}
      plugins={[sanitize(), linkAttributes()]}
    />;

    Validation

    renderReactNative throws StreamdReactNativeArgumentError when tokens is not an array.

    Web support

    Uses standard React Native primitives, so it works under react-native-web with the usual Vite / webpack alias. See apps/react-native-demo for a browser-hosted reference.

    License

    MIT.
